scheme_name: Pradhan Mantri Matsya Sampada Yojana (PMMSY-Inland): Establishment of Medium RAS Culture System Gujarat
page_title: Pradhan Mantri Matsya Sampada Yojana (PMMSY-Inland): Establishment of Medium RAS Culture System Gujarat

details: The scheme "Pradhan Mantri Matsya Sampada Yojana (PMMSY-Inland)" with a component Establishment of Medium RAS (with 6 tanks of minimum 30m3/tank capacity 10t/crop) Culture System, introduced by the Agriculture, Farmers Welfare and Co-operation Department, Government of Gujarat, aims to increase fish production by rearing fish in reservoirs and provide employment to people involved in inland fisheries activities. Through this scheme, financial assistance is provided to individuals and entities for the establishment of Recirculating Aquaculture System (RAS) based medium-scale fish tanks using limited land and water resources. The scheme is implemented by the Commissioner of Fisheries of the State Governments, with 60% contribution from the Government of India and 40% from the Government of Gujarat.
Assistance is provided for accelerating inland fish farming activities in freshwater bodies, increasing inland fish production and boosting inland fisheries.

Except for water bodies or ponds, in less land, less water, Biofloc with source as well, Recirculating Aquaculture System (RAS). By creating fish tanks with this method, more fish production can be obtained in less land and less water. If more beneficiaries get the benefit of the subsidy of this scheme, more fish production can be achieved with less water sources and less land.
Inland fish production capacity can be increased by the establishment of freshwater hatcheries.
benefits: The scheme provides assistance for the establishment of a Medium Recirculating Aquaculture System (RAS) with 6 tanks of a minimum 30m3/tank capacity (10t/crop).
The unit cost for this component is ₹25,00,000/- per unit.
General Category: 40% of the unit cost or 40% of the actual cost, whichever is less, is paid as subsidy.
Female/Scheduled Caste (SC)/Scheduled Tribe (ST) Category: 60% of the unit cost or 40% of the actual cost, whichever is less, is paid as subsidy.
The benefit for the establishment of a Medium RAS (with 6 tanks of minimum 30m3/tank capacity 10t/crop) culture system (PMMSY) can be reclaimed once in a lifetime.
eligibility: The applicant must be an individual, society, self-help group, association, reservoir lease holder, or private entity involved in inland fisheries activities.
The applicant must be above 18 years of age.
The applicant must have their own bank account.

The applicant must have their own private land or a minimum 10-year lease agreement for land to avail construction-related assistance on land. In case of co-owners, the consent form of co-owners must be submitted.
The applicant must have a Voter ID/Ration Card/Aadhar Card/Passport as proof of identity.
All categories (General, Women, Scheduled Caste, and Scheduled Tribe) will receive benefits under the Inland Schemes.
Scheduled Caste and Scheduled Tribe beneficiaries are required to have a Scheduled Caste/Scheduled Tribe certificate to avail benefits under the inland components.

online: The applicant may visit and apply online at the
Click on 'Login' from the top right-hand side, then click on "Beneficiary Registration" and select any one from the 'Farmer' or 'Institutional' or 'Except For the Farmer' as the Beneficiary Type.

Enter the 'Company Name' and Address if you have selected 'Institutional' or 'Except For the Farmer' as the Beneficiary Type. And for all select the District, Taluka, and Village, choose 'what type of account holder you are?', enter your Account Number and Name, solve the Captcha, and click 'Save and Next'.
Enter your Mobile Number and E-mail address, click 'Send OTP', enter the received OTP, create and confirm a Password, solve the Captcha, and click 'Submit'.
Click on 'Login' from the top right-hand side of the homepage. On the home page, click on ‘Schemes’ and then click on ‘Agricultural Schemes’.
Now select the scheme, click on the ‘Apply’ button, and an application page will open.
Click on the "Apply New" button and submit a new application.
Click on the "Update Application" button to add corrections to the application.
Once the application is done, confirm it.
Take a printout of the confirmed application.

documents_required: Voter ID/Ration Card/Aadhaar Card/Passport (as proof of identity)
Land Ownership Documents (if applicant has own private land)
Consent Form of Co-owners (in case of co-owned land)
Scheduled Caste/Scheduled Tribe Certificate (for Scheduled Caste and Scheduled Tribe beneficiaries)

faqs.question: Who can apply under this scheme?
faqs.answer: Individuals, societies, self-help groups, associations, reservoir leaseholders, and private entities involved in inland fisheries activities can apply under this scheme.

faqs.question: What type of fisheries activities are supported under this scheme?
faqs.answer: The scheme supports inland fish farming, especially using Recirculating Aquaculture Systems (RAS) for fish production in less land and water.

faqs.question: Is there any age requirement for availing the benefit of this scheme?
faqs.answer: Yes, the applicant must be above 18 years of age to be eligible.

faqs.question: Is it necessary to have land to apply for this scheme?
faqs.answer: Yes, the applicant must have either their own private land or land on a lease of at least 10 years.

faqs.question: Can a Scheduled Caste applicant avail any category-specific benefit?
faqs.answer: Yes, SC applicants must submit a valid caste certificate to avail benefits meant for their category.

faqs.question: Where can I apply for this scheme?
faqs.answer: Applications must be submitted online through the iKhedut portal at https://ikhedut.gujarat.gov.in.

faqs.question: What is the minimum tank size required for setting up RAS under this scheme?
faqs.answer: The scheme requires a setup of 6 tanks with each having a minimum capacity of 30m³.

faqs.question: What is the expected fish production per crop using RAS?
faqs.answer: The system should be capable of producing 10 tonnes per crop.

faqs.question: Is there any time limit for availing the benefit again?
faqs.answer: Yes, the benefit under this scheme can be availed only once in a lifetime.

faqs.question: Does the scheme promote the use of less land and water?
faqs.answer: Yes, the scheme encourages fish production using limited land and water through RAS technology.

faqs.question: Can I apply if I have leased land for fish farming?
faqs.answer: Yes, applicants with a minimum 10-year lease agreement on land are eligible for construction-related benefits.
